entry.title: Hiring Agents to reinforce your P2P team with SAP: When smart AI Agents become your new colleagues
entry.content:
Can you imagine an augmented team, working like a dream team, to help you negotiate, evaluate, and award an RFQ with your Vendors?  We're actively experimenting with this approach to bring the vision to reality for our clients.

Let’s start simple

We assume that today Personas are primarily working in a traditional manner, performing repetitive and time-consuming manual tasks across the various steps of the Procure-to-Pay (P2P) business process.

As an example, we can represent a portion of the P2P process like this:

People collaborate and communicate across departments and with their partner ecosystems, interacting with both SAP and non-SAP backend systems to gather the inputs needed to proceed with their tasks. These interactions are dynamic and iterative, driven by continuously evolving daily business needs.

Let’s assume we can now move a few boxes - representing the current 'as-is' process steps - from being 100% manual to something more advanced. This could involve elements from previous RPA investments, innovations from the new paradigm of Generative AI, or other emerging solutions. We can represent it like this:

What are we aiming for?

From RFQ creation to awarding and document generation, we envision a flow where different technologies can help in achieving:

  • Smart RFQ Creation: where e.g. SAP Joule Agent will suggest the most suitable Vendors. No panic, humans will make the final call.
  • Automated Response Processing: RPA e.g. from UiPath, and document recognition will handle heavy lifting.
  • Collaborative Evaluation: Agents based on different technologies (e.g., MyWave, Azure, others), and operating across departments such as Procurement, Production, and Finance, will analyze pricing and conditions, and evaluate related risks - working together in an Agent-to-Agent concerto.
  • Awarding Proposal: Gen-AI will suggest optimal awarding scenarios. You’ll validate them.
  • Document Automation: From award letters to purchase orders, everything will flow seamlessly.

Breaking silos, building a modern collaboration

With this flexible and scalable hyper-automated approach, we aim to break down traditional departmental silos. Information will flow seamlessly, decisions will be more informed, and response times will be drastically reduced. No worries about if colleagues are enjoying the beach during the summer or they are too busy to provide input - your needs will still be met.

Elevate Humans to focus on higher-value tasks

We are experimenting with Agents, autonomous or semi-autonomous, as new colleagues trained in the procurement business segment - such as in the automotive industry. These Agents support humans by using this solution to detect risks (e.g., identifying suppliers in conflict zones or with questionable labor practices), compare prices against market trends, and ensure production is not delayed due to logistics issues.

So, are you ready to hire your future digital colleagues?
I know you might be from the Order-to-Cash business process, right? This is a representation of the Procure-to-Pay process, but we're scaling across all business processes 😉

#EnjoyAI

entry.title: Re: Answers to common Yard Logistics questions
entry.content:
Hi Nancy An,

Very nice and helpful description of what and what not YL can do and how it integrates with different systems! Thanks very much!

 

BR Bas

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part II: Provisioning to BTP and S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello @evernite , @sam_venkat 

Normally you should be able to do that, maybe try to review the https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity/system-integration-guide/global-user-id-in-integration-scenarios for the conditions ? 

And these examples : https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity/system-integration-guide/step-by-step-example-for-setting-up-global-user-id

entry.title: Re: I Killed Fiori (And I'd Do It Again)
entry.content:
Hi @junwu 👋,

Tagged it fiori(ui) because that's where the pain lives—anyone in that tag is debugging why tiles won't load or decoding cryptic @ui annotations.

Yes, this logic could kill anything. I could replace email with smoke signals, but that misses the point. 😏

TRIZ principle: If something exists only to justify its own existence, eliminate it.

Fiori screens that wrap a single OData call in eight clicks aren't "user experience"—they're CSS cosplay. So:

  • Keep: Rich dashboards, complex approvals, anything adding genuine insight
  • Ax: CRUD pageants that turn API calls into click marathons

Some UIs earn their keep. Most are just expensive theater.

Cheers!

entry.title: Re: "Mastering the Application Rationalization with SAP L...
entry.content:
Hi all, 

we are working under huge pressure to update the learning journey with a new link to the demo system. Please try again in the next few hours after cleaning your browser cache.

Thank you,

Omar

entry.title: Re: Creating Mashup to Show SAP S/4HANA Addresses in SAP Service Cloud Version 2 for Utilities Add-o
entry.content:
@steen_hansen 

Thank you writing a detailed blog! 

In your blog noticed few points which can be improved further, images are too small and blur and code you have pasted in clear text can be moved into code snippet which displays in HTML language and looks pretty good.

entry.title: Re: Business Objects with Microsoft Fabric
entry.content:
Hi Brian,

Not clear how you configured the reports in BOE, you should be able to set default log on info for each report.

Is viewing the reports for all users failing, if so likely issue is the DB client is not configure for BOE to access, when you say ODBC is configure are you saying it's working on the BOE Server or only in the Report Designer?

Crystal reports fully supports DB security, for those who can't see the reports when you will need to do is either schedule the reports and allow those user who don't have access to view them or set the log on info as an administrator in Report and again set the log on info in BOE database setup etc.

There are other ways to allow them but you need to decide who can access the reports using various credentials etc.

Note - using Admin rights may give them access to modify the report SQL to get access to all data...

Don

entry.title: Re: SAP FIORI | Timestamp conversion in fiori front end
entry.content:
I believe 20250425051406 is local time at your backend

you have to converted it utc at backend, so odata can expose it correctly.

entry.title: Re: SAP Authorization and Trust Management Service - Authorization API: Role Collections all subacc
entry.content: I think Best Practice Architecture mentioned that XSUAA instance should be just one and this should be a shared service. I asked SAP expert yesterday at Global call the same question, but I have not yet received an answer. I will follow up

Current WD Component/View
ComponentSMUD_OBJECT_LIST
WindowW_MAIN
ViewV_OBJECT_LIST
Application ComponentSV-SMG-IMP-BIM

SAP Solution Manager  #Solution documentation

entry.title: Why is a portion of my annotation file not being recognized in Fiori adapation project?
entry.content:
Hello all, it seems I have run into a minor issue with a project I am working on (Fiori App: Perform Maintenance Jobs - F5104A) . I have provided a portion of the annotation file fragment that I have been working with. Everything seems to work fine in our Sandbox including previewing the application in terms of hiding standard fields, adding a new field, and rearranging the fields. With everything looking good, the team was ready to move forward with the local annotation file change but once we added it to the launchpad as a tile (still in our sandbox), the arrangement of the fields do not match what we initially arranged in BAS in preview. Is there are reason the field rearrangement works as expected in preview, but not when we create a tile for the new variant on the launchpad? When the tile is launched the app variant recognizes all the annotations but the rearrangement of fields.

When opening the application in our Sandbox environment from preview in BAS, it works as expected with the fields in the correct order:

After creating technical / business catalogs, our new tile containing the variant does not recognize the rearrangement of fields:

Annotation file with the rearranged fields:

Additionally, we went ahead to recreate this is in our development environment, and the rearrangement of fields does not even work in preview like it does in our Sandbox. 

Any ideas what's going on between preview, the launchpad, and systems? The code is entirely the same in both systems, and both previews do not show the same results. 

Please let me know!

Regards,

Patrick

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification Transformation: How You Can Get and Stay Certified
entry.content:
Dear @KarthikKalaimani please find here my recommendations for you:

1. Stay Current Eligibility: so when there was no chance to take the assessment within 12 month due to issues with subscription renewal the affected person can open a support ticket - but our teams are checking if there was a subscription issue. See Staying certified - FAQ question 7 "How do I transfer my certificates?" is explaining the process to request support on this one.

2.Meeting Minimum Certification Criteria: in all honesty, I cannot answer this one as this is a question more for the partner competency team but looking into the details here SAP Partner Portal | Requirements might help you

 

Kind Regards, Suzanne

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part II: Provisioning to BTP and S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi @FlorentTardivon @evernite @sam_venkat 

How you're planning to handle this scenario in case you have users also created from SSFF->IAS standard integration?

If the user comes initially from SSFF IAS will create a UUID automatically, do you think is possible to disable the attribute in SSFF integration or maybe work with "scope": "createEntity"  in the attributes?

entry.title: Re: How to add a calculated column in SAC story without s...
entry.content: Hey, to do that add a story calculation and use the formula %GrandTotal

entry.title: SAP DRC automation public cloud Netherlands
entry.content:
Hi all,

We are currently activating/configuring DRC in SAP public cloud.
In the Netherlands we can use this for automatic filing of VAT return and ECLS.

To enable this we need to provide an authentication file provided by a Trust Service Provider. But it is not clear to me which certificate is needed.
Any idea which one is needed?
Maybe there are more, but if I check the website of the RDI and select the link to the trusted providers I see a few "certificates" mentioned:
QeRDS
QCert for ESig
QCert for ESeal
QWAC
QTimestamp

Appreciate your support.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Re: SAP FIORI | Timestamp conversion in fiori front end
entry.content: That's the solution. So instead of concat(), use dats_tims_to_tstmp().

entry.title: Re: Getting error in SAP Integration Suite IFLOW - Unreso...
entry.content:
Hello Gopal,

So now you are able to get the data when you make two separate calls one is for token and another one is api(token value from previous call).

Now lets check what all are the curl values when passing to token API endpoint fetching the token ?

entry.title: Re: Unable to mass update product master data
entry.content:
• Missing Authorizations:

    • Ensure your user role has the necessary authorizations for mass changes. Check for roles including access to:

      • MM_PUR_MAINTAIN or similar authorization objects.

      • OData service /SAP/PRODUCT_MASS_PROC_SRV (depending on your system version).

  • Product Selection Criteria:

    • Verify that all selected products are valid and belong to the same product type (e.g., material type) or organization level.

    • Some inconsistencies (e.g., blocked materials or different product categories) can cause mass processing to fail.

entry.title: Re: Covert Date format from 00000 to Null value.
entry.content:
Hi,

Don't use separate fields for date and time in OData services, but use timestamps instead. Separate fields lead to multiple issues, like that the initial time (000000) is also a valid time (00:00:00) and gets ugly with timezone handling.

If you construct a timestamp out of initial GATE_DATE_OUT and GATE_TIME_OUT, then it will be shown as blank.

BR,
Alex

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio AI units
entry.content: Hello, thanks for your prompt & detailed answer. We would like to use the AI Units in order to upload documents and translate/transform them into BPMN diagramms. It is called (Text-to-process function). Is this function available with the AI Units or do we need another type of licences or Signavio Module? Thanks & BR, Angeliki

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part II: Provisioning to BTP and S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello @dyaryura,

Most of the time the UUID from SSFF is filled in the customAttribute1, so even if you update the global user id on the user file, the one on the customAttribute1 will not change.

Regards

entry.content:

Cannot connect to the Service on USAWSCONW113.us.deloitte.comWeb Service error:29A connection with the server could not be establishedWinHttpSendRequest failed in plugin_winhttpfsend() 

I have followed the note 2010805, but it didn't resolve the issue.

Hello All,

I added custom fields in ekko table and extended the CDS view used in Excel field setting. I am able to get the data when i download excel with my custom fields. But i am unable to make any update by uploading excel. Has anyone tried this?

entry.title: Re: Workflow Process & Enhancement on Routine maintenance...
entry.content:
1. Define User Status Profile (BS02)

  • Transaction Code: BS02

  • New Profile Name: ZPM_NOTIF_APPR

Status Definitions:

  • PEND - Pending Approval (Initial Status)

  • APPR - Approved

  • REJ - Rejected

Business Transaction Control:

  • PEND Status:

    • Set as initial status

    • Forbid NOCO (Notification Completion)

    • Optionally restrict ORCR (Order Creation) and task processing

  • APPR Status:

    • Permit all follow-up processing

  • REJ Status:

    • Forbid all follow-up processing

✅ Save and activate the status profile

2. Assign Status Profile to Notification Type

Path: SPRO → Plant Maintenance → Notifications → Define Notification Types

  1. Select notification type (e.g., M1 - Breakdown Notification)

  2. Assign status profile: ZPM_NOTIF_APPR

✅ Save configuration

3. Design Workflow Template (PFTC_INS)

  • Object Type: BUS2038 (PM Notification)

  • Triggering Event: CREATED

Workflow Structure:

  1. Decision Step: "Approve Maintenance Notification"

    • Recipients: Assigned HOD/Approver

    • Decision Options: Approve/Reject

  2. Outcome Branch:

    • Approve: Set status to APPR using FM STATUS_CHANGE_EXTERN

    • Reject:

      • Set status to REJ

      • Send message to creator via Send Mail step

✅ Activate and test template

4. Link Workflow to Notification Event (SWETYPV)

  • Object Type: BUS2038

  • Event: CREATED

  • Receiver Type: Your workflow template

✅ Activate event linkage

5. Define Agent Assignment

Option 1: Organizational Structure (PPOMW)

  1. Create/maintain organizational units

  2. Assign users to responsible positions

Option 2: Rule-Based Determination (PFAC)

  • Create rules based on:

    • Work Center

    • Planner Group

    • Functional Location

✅ Configure for dynamic approver assignment

6. Notification Configuration (Optional)

  1. Configure SAPConnect (SCOT) for emails

  2. Ensure user email addresses in SU01

  3. Add Send Mail steps in workflow for:

    • Rejection notifications with reason

    • Approval confirmations

entry.title: Re: Why is a portion of my annotation file not being recognized in Fiori adapation project?
entry.content: please share how your target mapping configuration looks like

entry.title: Re: SAP FIORI | Timestamp conversion in fiori front end
entry.content:
Thanks for your reply. I have tried with converting the timestamp to UTC using dats_tims_to_tstmp() but the front end is converting the timestamps.

 

       cast( dats_tims_to_tstmp( _SalesDoc.CreationDate,_SalesDoc. CreationTime, 'UTC', $session.client, 'NULL' )   as timestamp )          as CreatedAt,

entry.title: Re: SAP FIORI | Timestamp conversion in fiori front end
entry.content: Yes, this is the expected behavior, that the FE converts the timestamp. Is you timestamp really in UTC? What did you pass as the tzone parameter to dats_tims_to_tstmp()? It should likely be abap_system_timezone( $session.client,'NULL' ).

entry.title: Re: SAP FIORI | Timestamp conversion in fiori front end
entry.content: The line should be: cast( dats_tims_to_tstmp( _SalesDoc.CreationDate, _SalesDoc.CreationTime, abap_system_timezone( $session.client,'NULL' ), $session.client, 'NULL' ) as timestamp ) as CreatedAt,

entry.title: Re: SAP FIORI | Timestamp conversion in fiori front end
entry.content:
I have tried with the below statement . The timestamp is being added 5 hours when it got converted to the UTC timestamp and in fiori front end extra 5.5 hours are added. Is there any way to fix the issue.

 

cast( dats_tims_to_tstmp( _SalesDoc.CreationDate, _SalesDoc.CreationTime, abap_system_timezone( $session.client,'NULL' ), $session.client, 'NULL' ) as timestamp ) as CreatedAt,

entry.title: Re: Utilities and Tax Payments in Brazil
entry.content:
Hi All

I'm getting an error as "Payment method E cannot be used for Tax/Utilities payments"

Message no. IDFI_BR_PAYM009

System Hana2021

When i give bar code in bank ref field w/o bar code it get processed in F110 however with bar code i get the error.

What specific config is im missing in FBZP or else that proposal run gets in above error

Highly appreciate if get an insight

Thank you Jeremy for your answer. 

Since the "Reclassify Payables and Receivables" tile is not available in my system, I have used the "Schedule General Ledger Jobs" app which offers a template that suits my customer needs.

entry.content:
Hello Ken,

as stated by @MarcioBlos, the "Preliminary requirement for WBS" is creating the PR without the reservation for "L" stock category, so that is satisfying the requirement.
The only downside is that this material component consumption cannot be planned but we are not doing planning so that's ok

entry.title: EFD REINF Erro MS0038
entry.content:
Ola 
Estamos enfrentando um erro ao enviar todos eventos do REINF, quando enviamos o XML na tag <TpAmb> esta marcado como 1 porem estamos recebendo o XML de retorno com a Mensagem de erro que o ambiente esta setado como 2, estamos utilizando para comunicação o BTP e o DRC cloud edition, certificados atualizados, ambiente de qualidade esta enviando os testes e as respostas estão vindo corretamente

Alguém mais passou por esse problema?

Obrigada

entry.title: build error on Build apps runtime 4.15.24
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
I'm getting the error below while trying to build my appgyver web app.
The error is occurring even in versions previously build perfectly in the past.

Hope the team find a solution. Thanks! 

Build Log
AB version: 3.30.1
COMMAND: 

USER_METERING_ENABLED=true SHOW_SUBSCRIPTION_VERIFICATION_NOTIFICATION=false SUBSCRIPTION_VERIFICATION_NOTIFICATION_DISMISSIBLE=true NEXT_SUPPORT_RELATIVE_PATHS=true NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=2000 --no-experimental-global-navigator  npxrnv export -p web -s standalone -c standalone --ci --yes --packageManager yarn 

FAILED with ERROR: 

warn: You have integration @sba-runtime/integration-builder defined, but it wasn't found in package.json. ERR: Error: Cannot find module '../renative.json'
Require stack:
- ./node_modules/@sba-runtime/integration-builder/lib/config.js
- ./node_modules/@sba-runtime/integration-builder/lib/tasks/taskExport.js
- ./node_modules/@sba-runtime/integration-builder/lib/index.js
- ./node_modules/@rnv/core/lib/modules/index.js
Error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, open '/app/.appgyver/app/renative.private.json'
 at Object.openSync (node:fs:561:18)
 at Object.readFileSync (node:fs:445:35)
 at handleCryptoApiKeys (/app/current/buildHooks/dist/index.js:29381:56)
 at /app/current/node_modules/@rnv/core/src/buildHooks/index.ts:21:50
 at Object.dynatraceRegularInvoke [as doInvoke] (/opt/dynatrace/oneagent-paas/agent/bin/1.311.79.20250515-130846/any/nodejs/nodejsagent.js:4087:22)
error: ⨯ ○ export: COMMAND: 
node --max-old-space-size=12000 /app/current/node_modules/next/dist/bin/next build 
FAILED with ERROR: 
> Build failed because of webpack errors
Error: Command failed with exit code 1: node --max-old-space-size=12000 /app/current/node_modules/next/dist/bin/next build
 ⚠ Invalid next.config.js options detected: 
 ⚠ Invalid input at "env.supportRelativePaths"
 ⚠ Expected string, received boolean at "env.supportRelativePaths"
 ⚠ Expected undefined, received boolean at "env.supportRelativePaths"
 ⚠ Invalid input at "env.appSwitcherTests"
> Build failed because of webpack errors
Attention: Next.js now collects completely anonymous telemetry regarding usage.
This information is used to shape Next.js' roadmap and prioritize features.
You can learn more, including how to opt-out if you'd not like to participate in this anonymous program, by visiting the following URL:
https://nextjs.org/telemetry

 at handlePromise (/app/current/node_modules/execa/index.js:118:26)
 at processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:105:5)
 ⚠ Invalid next.config.js options detected: 
 ⚠ Invalid input at "env.supportRelativePaths"
 ⚠ Expected string, received boolean at "env.supportRelativePaths"
> Build failed because of webpack errors

entry.title: Re: Migration story of 48 SAP SuccessFactors HCM successful integrations in 4 Months
entry.content:
Hi, @KavyaManohara  thank for your share. An typicial example for integration best practices.

What particularly impresses me is your comprehensive utilization of SAP SuccessFactors Integration Center (IC) templates for all integrations ranging from simple to moderately complex scenarios.​​

In most customer environments, there is a strong demand for integration architecture standardization and simplification—typically achieved through API-based integration as the unified approach.

​That said, I believe the Integration Center represents an often-underestimated yet highly valuable tool.​​ It offers several key advantages:

  • ​Cost-effectiveness
  • ​No-code implementation​
  • ​Full control by SF consultants​
  • ​Flexible adaptability within defined scope​

These characteristics make IC particularly suitable for scenarios where rapid deployment and minimal technical overhead are priorities.

entry.title: How to view audit logs ?
entry.content:
We need to identify how to view audit logs for changes made by super users (administrator users) in SAP, or, failing that, have a record to review changes made by administrator users.
Please tell us how we obtained this information.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Delete future dated records and dates in job relationship
entry.content:
Hello.

if someone mistakenly uploaded future dated record in job relationship portlet in production and now how to delete future dated records and dates in Job relationship portlet in SuccessFactors? i have tried via delimit however it is just relationship got removed but future dates still shown. i tried via delete how that option doesn't support the system.

Could you please help me to understand how we can delete the future dated records and dates in job relationship portlet via import?

entry.title: Re: How to view audit logs ?
entry.content:
Please use the apps

Display Change Documents https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('CA_SHOW_CD')/S34

Display Static System Audit https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F5461')/S34

Display Security Audit Log v2 https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F4204A')/S34

entry.title: Are there any substitutions for WorkZone and S4HANA Cloud when using the BTP Consumption Monitor?
entry.content:
Hi everyone, I recently successfully created/implemented the BTP Consumption Monitor as shown by Willem Pardaens in the YouTube video linked to the Discovery Mission. I personally used WorkZone advanced edition and S4HANA Cloud for it. However, I have a bit of a 2-part question.

1. Is it possible to integrate with or use the Fiori Launchpad service as the front end instead of WorkZone (standard or advanced) since a lot of potential clients already use the Fiori Launchpad service?

2. Are there any substitutes for S4HANA Cloud? (my subaccount was in a region where the S4HANA Cloud free trial was not available so I used the paid version. While that wasn't necessarily a big problem for me, potential clients might also be in a region where the free HANA Cloud trial is also not available and want to use something else)

entry.title: Re: Receive supplier invoices from authorities' platform and send them to different automation solut
entry.content:
Hi @Pritish_Saha , 

Thanks for looking into this and guiding me. I noticed that , we have already completed the advised configuration.

Could you pls confirm, what else could be checked and configured to have the push button - 'Create Incoming invoice" in the section - KSEF Incoming invoices.

entry.title: How to get 10000 developers to securely deliver their software
entry.content:
Let’s start with a few numbers: SAP S/4HANA contains more than 300 million lines of code, the software runs more than 90% of the world’s largest corporations and, most importantly for the scope of this article, it currently keeps about 10.000 developers at SAP busy. Those developers are spread all over the globe, covering pretty much every time zone. In this challenging setup, how do you ensure the secure delivery of software? Short answer: transparency. Long answer: continue reading.

Obviously, the answer is more complex than that. In ordert o ensure the security of a software that complex with that many developers in a decentralized setup, quite a few measures have to be in place in order to ensure securely written code and applications. These issues are addressed by SAP, as we have implementes a „**bleep**-Left“ approach to security early on, including a product standard for security, a secure development and operations lifecycle (we need to secure our cloud environments, too), and, of course various tools and trainings to support developers in developing secure code in the first place.

Nevertheless, with 10.000 people working on the code daily, overseeing the security posture of the written code can be challenging, to say the least. This is why we have come up with the „Security Execution Dashboard“. At this point, we have to make a confession: there’s not much new stuff in the dashboard – but as with a lot of good things, it’s the combination of existing features and functions that make the dashboard so powerful – and is one of the reasons it won the prestigious CSO50 award this year, making it the second year in a row that SAP was able to win this particular award. Let’s deep dive into what made the security execution dashboard award-winning.

One of the biggest technical hurdles was the sheer diversity of tools involved. SAP’s security landscape includes a mix of industry-standard scanners like GitHub, Blackduck, and Checkmarx, as well as SAP’s own tools like FioriDAST. Each of these tools speaks its own language—different data formats, different APIs, different update cycles. To make sense of this chaos, the team built a unified data extraction framework. Think of it as a translator that sits between the dashboard and each tool, standardizing the way data is pulled, parsed, and processed. Every tool got its own connector or adapter, ensuring that no matter how the data looked coming in, it would be clean and consistent going out.

But pulling the data was only half the battle. The next challenge was making it meaningful. Security data is only useful if it’s tied to the right team, the right product, and the right release. That meant building a harmonized data model that could map scan results to specific areas of responsibility. This wasn’t just a matter of matching names—it required defining mapping rules, building validation mechanisms, and ensuring that the same issue wasn’t counted twice or missed entirely. The result is a dashboard that doesn’t just show you what’s wrong—it shows you who needs to fix it.

Performance was another sticking point. Querying live data from all these tools in real time would have brought the system to its knees. So the team implemented caching and temporary in-memory storage to keep things fast and responsive. Frequently accessed data is now stored locally, reducing the load on source systems and ensuring that developers aren’t left waiting for their dashboards to load.

Data quality also proved to be a recurring issue. In the early days, scan results were often misattributed or incomplete, making it hard to trust the insights. To fix this, the team introduced a robust data quality management process. Automated validation checks, regular audits, and feedback loops with tool owners helped tighten the accuracy of the data and improve the reliability of team mappings.

And then there was the human side. Convincing thousands of developers to adopt a new tool—especially one that shines a light on security gaps—is no small feat. The team tackled this with a mix of early engagement, pilot programs, and a strong internal communications push. Over time, the dashboard earned its place as the go-to source for security insights, not by mandate, but by proving its value.

Today, the Security Execution Dashboard is more than just a reporting tool. It’s a living system that adapts to the needs of SAP’s engineering teams. It’s fast, it’s accurate, and it’s deeply integrated into the way SAP builds software. And most importantly, it’s helped shift the conversation around security—from something that happens after the fact, to something that’s embedded from the very first line of code.

entry.title: Re: Blog It Forward - Marssel Vilaça
entry.content: Impressive. Kudos for maintaining the work life balance while leading teams and working on emerging technologies . 🙂

entry.title: Re: SAP FIORI | Timestamp conversion in fiori front end
entry.content: Did you check that the content of CreatedAt is really the timestamp in UTC? If then your browser is set to Indian time zone, then the result is the expected one.

entry.title: Re: Can someone provide the contact details of "Digital L...
entry.content:
Hi @Milind,

the correct link is:
https://d.dam.sap.com/s/p/a/jzE1zVY/50157011_Partner%20services%20for%20RISE%20with%20SAP_V5_Service_One-Page_EN_XX_Functional_design_review.pdf?rc=10&inline=true&doi=SAP883761

Regards.
Marcelo Pinheiro

entry.title: Inquiry Regarding Email Storage Location in S4 Client Profile
entry.content:
Hello,

In S4, within the client profile, in the "Address" tab, I am looking for the table where email addresses are stored, which seem to be listed under the "Address Independent Communication" section. Can you confirm this or indicate precisely where to find them?

Thank you,

Best regard

entry.title: Re: SAP FIORI | Timestamp conversion in fiori front end
entry.content: can you create a new order to see what is saved in db? what is your system time zone?

entry.title: Re: SAP FIORI | Timestamp conversion in fiori front end
entry.content:
The system time zone is CST

User time zone is UTC.

I'm working on building an SAP CDS view that includes both date and time fields, along with additional data. The goal is for users to filter records based on a selected date range and a time range, and retrieve only the entries that match both conditions.

 Example:

Column1 Date Time
Record120.06.202511:15:55
Record221.06.202511:14:56
Record322.06.202512:15:16
 

Assuming the user selects:

  • Date range: 20.06.2025 to 22.06.2025

  • Time range: 11:20:00 to 12:20:00

The CDS view should return only the records that fall within both of those ranges. Based on the example above, the expected output should be:

Column1 Date Time
Record221.06.202511:14:56
Record322.06.202512:15:16

To achieve this I am converting date and time into one timestamp field .

entry.title: Production Schedule Profile field is not visible in Product Master Data App
entry.content:
We are facing issue where we are unable to maintain "Production Schedule Profile" field in Manage Product Master Data App. 

 

How can we add it ?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Production Schedule Profile field is not visible in P...
entry.content:
Please use the Adapt UI in the app Manage Product Master Data to add the field Prod. Sched. Profile

 

Please refer to the resource Adapt UI that explains how to add the field and list of fields available

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps/Process Automation: "Workflow Definiti...
entry.content:
Hello all, 

The solution provided is correct. 

SAP Note contained one dot, reason why was not being displayed/found correctly. 

Here is the correct link of the SAP Note

If one follows the instructions described to include an API Key in the header it will work. Or deploying the Project in the Public Environment. 

Probably everyone gets confused when the deployment action is being performed because the "Public" enviroment is market for deprecated and people deploy into another environment instead. 

To access that environment, it requires the API Key. 

Hope it helps. 

Thank you

entry.title: Re: API for add new Pricing Conditions to an Existing Pur...
entry.content: Hi Jagoda, the problem is not in the pricing and values. That's a technical problem because API cannot support 'POST' Operation

entry.title: Re: SAP FIORI | Timestamp conversion in fiori front end
entry.content: you didn't answer the first question? we know what you want, first we have to check what you have now, then we will try to figure out getting what you want

entry.title: Cannot change or update the 'Without Qty Posting' field in the Return Request document.
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I’m encountering an issue in SAP Business One version 10 FP 2502 and FP 2505. When creating a Return Request document, I’m able to check or uncheck the Without Qty Posting option. However, once the Return Request is saved, the system no longer allows me to update this field—I cannot check or uncheck it anymore.

It’s important to note that the Return Request document is not based on another document; it was created independently. I tested this same scenario in earlier versions, and in those cases I was able to modify the Without Qty Posting checkbox after saving.

 
Is there any setting or option available in these versions that could help resolve this?

entry.content: I would check Query preview first to see if it extracts the data on the third session at all

entry.title: ECC to BPC
entry.content:
Dear All,

We aim to establish a direct mapping of GL accounts from ECC to BPC (Consolidations). Could you please specify which fields within the GL master record indicate that an account is configured to flow into BPC? Our goal is to identify the relevant account attributes in ECC GL Master that determine whether an account is included in BPC.

Current process: ECC - Anaplan - BPC

Anaplan is converting the GL data apser BPC requirement and we are thinking to get rid of Anaplan 

Below alternative way we tried but not suits our requirements:

Custom table replace to Anaplan

Alternative account number in GL master

Group COA

Thank

entry.title: Re: xercesc_3_2::RuntimeException Error while loading SAP...
entry.content: Thank you, Don. My application is a legacy C++/MFC MDI application that heavily relies on DAO and Crystal Reports 8.5. Unfortunately, due to architectural constraints and dependencies, it cannot be migrated to 64-bit at this time. Given this, I'm currently limited to using the 32-bit Crystal Reports runtime, and I understand that support for it is expected to end in December 2025. I'm exploring options for maintaining compatibility in the short term while planning a longer-term migration strategy. Any suggestions or shared experiences from others in a similar situation would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: SAP FIORI | Timestamp conversion in fiori front end

then why can't you figure out what's wrong?

entry.title: Re: ATC - How to Implement AUTHORITY-CHECK
entry.content: Thank you very much Laura. It worked for me. Regards

entry.title: Re: Why is a portion of my annotation file not being reco...
entry.content:
Thanks for the clarification.

I would check the following:

1) I'm not so sure that in this scenario you would need to fill the ICF path in App manager (in red below) as you have already the Component ID (in green below). As this is an easy change, I'd try clearing the ICF path field inside the technical catalog in App manager and testing it again. If you have used references in your business catalogs there is no change needed on them.

2) It's possible to see that you have create a different combination of semantic object + action in your adapted version of the app - which I believe it's a good idea.
Standard app: #MaintenanceJob-performJob
Adapted custom app: #MaintenanceJobz-zperformJobTest

Can you confirm that when  testing in your S/4HANA system (not in BAS) your user has access only to the Adapted custom app? 

If you have used the same semantic object + action I bet that the launchpad could lead to inconsistencies but it's another test worth doing. In order to achieve that you cannot have both apps in the same catalog. You might want to delete the reference for the standard app from your business catalog to test that. Make sure your user do not have access to the standard app by means of another catalog.

entry.title: Re: Why is a portion of my annotation file not being reco...
entry.content:
Hi @junwu ! So in the MIME report in SE80 we have 1.108, preview is 1.136, and version in FLP with the app variant is 1.108. When I created the project I chose the latest version instead of the system version (1.108).

Should I have chosen the system version? This is new for me so I figured latest would be best.

Please let me know!

Regards, Patrick

entry.title: Introducing: Release Navigator for SAP Cloud ERP Private featuring 2023 FPS03
entry.content:
Stop Avoiding Releases & Embrace Cloud Innovation

We acknowledge that upgrading ERP technology presents a complex and time-intensive challenge. It is inherent that ensuring seamless integration of new innovations with existing technical APIs and third-party software requires diligent preparation. Unlike the swift upgrades associated with cellular devices, ERP Private technology necessitates a more intricate approach. Otherwise, organizations utilizing ERP Private solutions would readily adopt the latest innovations upon releases.

In response to these challenges, SAP Enterprise Support has introduced the release navigator for SAP Cloud ERP Private. This comprehensive digital library offers up-to-date release information and enablement content, strategically organized for ease of access. The release navigator provides valuable guidance on release timelines, introducing insights into new feature enablement, and supports the change management process. By staying informed on upcoming releases, organizations are better equipped to navigate the complexities of ERP Private software upgrades. Take a look today!

 

Fictional Use Case

Situation #1: Globex, a fictional multinational enterprise, operates across several industries such as manufacturing, supply chain, and procurement, leveraging distinct lines of business (LoBs). To manage its complex operations, Globex has decided to implement SAP Cloud ERP Private. As the enterprise anticipates an upcoming system upgrade, it is imperative for Globex to access detailed release information and enablement content associated with the new features. This proactive approach will mitigate potential system disruptions and ensure that the organization fully capitalizes on the benefits of the latest innovations across its operations.

 

Resolution #1: By leveraging the release navigator, Globex efficiently accesses the Release Calendar located on the Product Specific page (see Figure 1: Product Specific page) to track upcoming product releases. Additionally, Globex benefits from curated release content and enablement resources, specifically tailored for manufacturing, supply chain, and procurement lines, conveniently organized under the Lines of Business tab. This streamlined approach accelerates the process of locating and scaling the necessary resources to comprehend the features being adopted.

 Figure 1: Product Specific page

Situation #2: Globex needs to be able to point new employee's to the latest information on the tool and does not have time to walk them through how to use it.

Resolution #2: Globex can access integrated content and enablement features directly in the release navigator, providing valuable resources for their organization. Each main page features a blue light bulb icon that users can select to access additional information and a guided tour of that page. Furthermore, new employees can be directed to the Get Assistance page located within the side menu (refer to Figure 2: Get Assistance page). This page includes a link to a curated blog tag within the SAP Community, offering access to a collection of blogs specifically related to the release navigator. Additionally, new employees will have access to a authenticated version of the tool that supports over 40 languages for translation. The translation feature can be activated via the shell bar, located at the top right corner of each page.

Figure 2: Get Assistance page

 

Connect with Us

We value your input and encourage you to share any feedback for specific content requests for future updates to the release navigator. Please feel free to contribute your suggestions in the Comments section below this post. You can also leave your feedback directly in the release navigator by selecting the User Feedback inside the side menu (see Figure 3: User Feedback page).

 Figure 3: User Feedback page

 

Always Bring Your Best,

Andrew Ford

entry.title: Re: Why is a portion of my annotation file not being reco...
entry.content: that's probably the problem, switch your bas to lower version to see if the problem is reproduced, very likely the function you are expecting is not implemented in lower version.

entry.title: Re: Inquiry Regarding Email Storage Location in S4 Client...
entry.content:
Email addresses are stored in ADR6 (whether this is universal I cannot confirm).

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: Why is a portion of my annotation file not being reco...
entry.content: If you deploy your app in the S/4HANA system (which I guess is the only way possible in your scenario as it's an adaptation project) the SAPUI5 version used when you open the app is always the SAPUI5 version available in the S/4HANA system (1.108 as you are in S/4HANA 2022). If you would deploy your app in BTP (which again, I don't think it's possible for adaptations) you can use any SAPUI5 version you want which is available in SAP's CDN. It's true that when you preview your app from BAS, it uses the latest SAPUI5 version by default. You can test in different versions in BAS by changing your ui5.yaml file or by using run configurations. But again, in this scenario you are stuck on 1.108. Testing in BAS using this version helps understanding where the issue is but it will not fix the issue unless the bug is in SAPUI5 itself. In that is the case the best you can do is upgrading SAPUI5 patch to the most recent one avaialble 1.108.44 is the latest) See https://ui5.sap.com/versionoverview.html

entry.title: Re: HTML5 app not showing in BTP Cockpit After deployed
entry.content:
Hello!

Please set this parameter to true and run again, maybe it solves the issue: 

HTML5Runtime_enabled: true

If it doesn't work, I would recommend deleting the mta.yaml file and generate it again.

entry.title: Partial Refund process in FICA
entry.content:
Hi,

Can anyone please help me on Partial refund process for FICA customer?

if there is a credit of $120.00- on account and out of this only $100.00 needs to be refunded .is there any way user can select to refund only $100.00?

Manual refund: Customer has a credit on account due to overpayment. But before requesting the refund, that credit is cleared new invoice. In this case a debit entry is posted to customer account, and the document number is sent to accounting to create a manual check.

entry.title: Re: Why is a portion of my annotation file not being reco...
entry.content:
You guys are awesome with your responses.

So, it looks like the versions are the issue... I changed the YAML to use 1.108.38 (system version), the field rearrangement does not work, then I tried with 1.108.44 which also does not work, then went back 1.136.2 and it works... that's a big bummer at the moment..

Regards,

Patrick

Thanks Samir,

But i got the below error now.

system=NSQ_NEW,

time=2025-06-27T11:47:29.824+00,

error=Source path: $.displayName is not available in content, but is required in defined system transformation Caused by: Source path: $.displayName is not available in content, but is required in defined system transformation Caused by: No results for path: $['displayName'],

content=<hidden content> - Set property ips.trace.failed.entity.content=true to display the content of failed entities

entry.title: Re: Why is a portion of my annotation file not being reco...
entry.content: no warry, you can use valuehelp to define the column you want

entry.title: Re: Blog It Forward - Marssel Vilaça
entry.content: Thank you,  @Vaibhav-Mishra !

entry.title: Re: Can we enforce "Move currently assigned Position belo...
entry.content: I wish SAP would come back with a solution here. Our company requires any replacement positions to go through workflow approval. The default toggle allows managers to transfer employees to another manager therefore creating new positions disguised as manager changes. For instance, a manager with 4 direct reports transfers their 4 employees to another manager. System creates 4 new positions under the new manager and keep 4 vacant positions under the current manager. So now there are 8 positions in total. We are looking to have the default toggle = Yes for managers but the code doesnt seem to exist

entry.title: Re: SAP Stammtisch in Mannheim (2 of 2) Drinks only - Let's chat Joule and all things SAP...
entry.content:
Looking forward to it!  A wonderful location.

Regarding
> A wonderful place for drinks (no food offered at location so eat earlier)

That's true, but just in case: 
On the other side of the street there is a very nice Falafel (Food) option to go or to sit.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Team Spotlight: Moshe Naveh - June 2025 Edition
entry.content: I’ve known Moshe since the old SCN times and had the chance to meet him in person. He’s always had a real talent for bringing people together in the community. Great interview!

entry.title: SAP Document Reporting Compliance Nigeria
entry.content:
We are working with the FIRS team on our SAP Document Reporting Compliance Nigeria Solution which is discussed below:

How It Works

  • ERP or API Integration: Businesses can integrate their existing Enterprise Resource
    Planning (ERP) systems with the MBS via API connectors or middleware plugins. This
    integration ensures seamless transmission of invoices from back-end systems directly to
    the FIRS validation hub.
  • Invoice Generation & Validation:
    • For B2B/B2G transactions, invoices are submitted to FIRS before being shared with the
      buyer. The invoice undergoes validation and receives a unique IRN, CSID, and a QR code
      that ensures authenticity and traceability.
    • For B2C invoices, the supplier issues the invoice immediately to the customer and must
      upload it to the MBS within 24 hours for reporting compliance.
    • Storage & Auditability: All validated invoices are stored in the MBS repository, where
      both suppliers and buyers, as well as the FIRS, can retrieve them for audit, reconciliation, or
      VAT verification. 

FIRS has given a number of use cases which leads to solution options for clients :

Core Use Cases & Capabilities

  • B2B / B2G Invoicing (Continuous Transaction Controls): This use case supports digital
    exchange of invoices between businesses or government agencies. Each invoice must be
    validated in real-time by the FIRS Merchant Buyer Solution (MBS) system, receiving a
    unique Invoice Reference Number (IRN), a Cryptographic Stamp (CSID), and a QR code for
    authentication.

In the above scenario's a business can choose the following:

1) Register their ERP as an access point and connect direct to the FIRS system; while this is possible during our discussions with FIRS this is not the prefered solution

2) Use a certified access point and connect your ERP to this as with other PEPPOL solution, one of FIRS goals is also to understand cross border invoice flows and by connecting to the PEPPOL network FIRS will then be reported on overseas customer invoices and overseas vendor invoices recieved via the access point.

  • Crimson-Partners Access Point is already registered in Nigeria as per our other solutions and can provide this for you
  • A company can choose their own access point and our solution will connect to this

B2C Invoicing (Near‑real‑time Reporting): Retail transactions involving consumers must
be reported within 24 hours. These invoices are issued directly to customers and
subsequently uploaded to the MBS platform for compliance.

  • Working with FIRS we have discussed using a consolidated invoice process daily to achieve this reporting for those with B2C sales

  • Standardized Formats & Access Points: Nigeria has adopted the UBL (Universal Business
    Language) XML standard to ensure interoperability. Invoices must be transmitted through
    licensed Access Point Providers (APPs) certified by NITDA. These APPs provide secure and
    traceable communication channels between senders and receivers via the PEPPOL 4-corner
    model. 

FIRS also provides an API to check the TIN validity of your customer before submitting invoicing accurately

✅ Benefits for Nigerian Organizations

  • Improved Compliance: Real-time invoice validation eliminates the risks of underreporting
    or delayed VAT submissions.
  • Enhanced Transparency: The use of QR codes and digital cryptographic stamps ensures
    each invoice is traceable, tamper-proof, and verifiable by both buyer and tax authority.
  • Operational Efficiency: Automation reduces manual processing, lowers administrative
    overhead, and facilitates faster transaction processing.
  • Accurate VAT Reporting: Invoice data is automatically captured and structured for VAT
    compliance, reducing the need for manual declarations.

⚖ Legal Mandate & Penalties
The implementation of electronic invoicing is legally mandated by the FIRS under the
Finance Act, supported by regulatory frameworks from NITDA and NCC. Non-compliance
can lead to penalties including monetary fines, interest on underreported taxes, and
restrictions on eligibility for input VAT claims.

� Rollout Strategy & Timeline

  • July 2025: Mandatory for large taxpayers as classified by FIRS.
  • Q4 2025: Gradual onboarding of medium-sized businesses.
  • 2026: Full rollout to small businesses and B2C operations.

Crimson provide a standard SAP Document Reporting Compliance Solution, a full staging area for testing, training and handbooks for your organisation

Benefits :

  • Single Global Process using Document Reporting Compliance
  • E2E compliant solution built with the FIRS team
  • Generation of electronic invoices in XML format (FI, SD) for B2B and B2G
  • Self-billing scenario's
  • FICA and RE-FX billing
  • Manual or automated invoice submission
  • Receiving of e-invoices in XML format and Integration to VIM Software
  • Human Readable PDF in Cockpit for incoming invoices

� Next Steps

  • Confirm your registration with FIRS and verify your Tax Identification Number (TIN).
  • Engage with a licensed Access Point Provider (APP) or Software Integrator (SI) to assist
    with system integration such as Crimson who can meet both these
  • Prepare your ERP systems or point-of-sale platforms for API connectivity and XML invoice
    generation.
  • Conduct end-to-end testing with FIRS and ensure your teams are trained in the e-invoicing
    process and legal requirements. 

� Bottom Line
The FIRSMBS e-invoicing system marks a major milestone in Nigeria’s digital tax
transformation. It also marks a shift for African countries with other African nations choosing highly complex solutions, Nigeria has chosen a global standard and is following in similar fashion to Malaysia, UAE and other upcoming mandates.

It ensures:

  • Real-time transparency
  • Reduced invoicing errors
  • Automated VAT reporting
  • Lower compliance costs

Compatible with ECC, S/4HANA & S/4 Cloud, this is another step on the journey of global electronic invoicing across borders.

Crimson Tax Tech | FIRS e-Invoicing Advisory

entry.title: Re: Integrating Microsoft Services with SAP Build Process Automation using Microsoft 365 SDK & G

entry.title: Re: Onboarding - Learning integration for Pre-day1 New Hires

Hi Samita, 

It mentions to bring student ID (STUD_ID), but the field Student ID is not avaialbe in the entity ONB2Process.

Regards, 
Suhaib

entry.title: Is there a SAP Module that is able to maintain lab equipment outside the plant maintenance area?
entry.content: We are looking for a way to maintain quality lab equipment, calibrations etc that is not planned/maintained by a Maintenance Planner.  Meaning the Quality Associates would need to plan when service vendors arrive on site to calibrate scales specifically located in their lab area.  How is this area maintained if Plant Maintenance will not pick up that work?  thank you,

entry.title: Restricting Access at Storage Location Level in IM & EWM
entry.content:
Hi,

In our current SAP S/4HANA setup, user access is controlled at the plant level for Inventory Management (IM) and at the warehouse level for Extended Warehouse Management (EWM).

We are exploring the possibility of implementing more granular restrictions at the storage location level within both IM and EWM modules. Has anyone implemented such restrictions, or is there a recommended approach (e.g., using authorizations, enhancements, or config settings) to achieve this?

Appreciate your insights or experiences!

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Create just Purchase Requisition for Stock Material f...
entry.content:
In the standard configuration using the PFV proc. ind. is Non-Stock and generates a PR and no reservation.  I copied PFV and made it L, which resulted in a reservation and no PR.  

Please show your config to get the results you wanted.   And what is your business process to consume the inventory?

entry.title: Re: Free SAP Certification and practice systems for students & lecturers
entry.content:
I have sent you required information l kindly review.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: ABAP ADT parallel locking issue for ABAP Classes
entry.content: This is a super helpful idea, we are finally converting over to Eclipse with ADT to make use of Github Copilot and I just ran into this today. Will test this out! Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Joule answers in eclipse
entry.content:
Hello,

Thanks for the answer. In some tests it got better with the sentence. But especially when programming, I never want to have invented certain specifications. In other words, I expected that one of the advantages of Joule over other llm's is that I don't have to specify such sets. Is it possible to set this as a default? Or even better, ATC variants according to which it should be based?

Best regards

Stefan

entry.title: How can I execute iRPA automation from the build App
entry.content:
Hi, 

I'm quite new to the iRPA with Build Process Automation.  I am hoping I can get some guidance here.  I have built an automation that can read excel file and execute a transaction in the backend.  It's working fine when testing the automation directly in BPA.  I have 3 input parameters in this automation.  My question is, how can an end user uses this automation, manually input the value and execute the automation?  My initial thought was to use BuildApp as a wrapper app and trigger the automation.  However, I have tried to configure the Destination, publish the automation, created a frame in build App with input fields.  However, I couldn't find the automation (process) in the published library, and hence, cannot link the build app with automation.  I am not sure if there is anything I've done incorrectly , or is there other solution ?  I'd appreciate any guidance/insights.  Thanks in advance !!

entry.title: Re: SAP CI-DS in SAP BTP Cloud foundry
entry.content:
Hi Malathi

Currently all CPI-DS servers are hosted on the Neo environment, where these will all be migrated to the Kyma environment in the future (see https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration-for-data-services/tenant-migration-guide/link-migration-waves).

There is no option to have the CPI-DS servers/tenants hosted on other environments.

Regards,
Ian
SAP Support

entry.title: Failed to load UI5 component for navigation intent "#SalesOrder-manageV2"
SAP Cloud ALM has now provisioned its 10,000th customer tenant for our valued customers!
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM, our cloud-native solution designed to support the entire lifecycle of SAP applications, has now provisioned its 10,000th customer tenant to our valued customers. Since its release on May 29, 2020, with just 10 beta customers, SAP Cloud ALM has demonstrated extraordinary growth, more than doubling its customer base each year. Growing from 250+ customers at the end of 2020 to an impressive 8,000+ last year, we now proudly celebrate the 10,000th customer tenant. This milestone highlights our customers’ demand for an effective and efficient cloud-based application lifecycle management solution that can be used with confidence to get the maximum benefit from their SAP solutions. 

As we celebrate this milestone, our journey with SAP Cloud ALM is far from over. While we have made significant progress, the growing user base provides SAP with a wealth of feedback and insights to continuously improve SAP Cloud ALM’s features and services to better meet our customers’ needs. Based on customer demand, we focused our efforts in three critical areas: 

First, we recognized the importance of seamless integration with other widely used solutions through open APIs for products like Tricentis test tools, JIRA, ServiceNow, Azure DevOps, Grafana, and others. This approach allowed SAP Cloud ALM to become an integral part of the integrated toolchain for the SAP Business Suite, providing the capability of Business Transformation with end-to-end visibility and management for implementing, deploying, operating, and continuously improving SAP solutions to reach a Clean Core. SAP Cloud ALM is now interconnected in a logical way with SAP Signavio, SAP LeanIX, SAP EnableNow, Tricentis test tools, and Synthi to take advantage of each platform’s strengths and optimize the implementation of new solutions. Deutsche Bahn is planning to adopt this business transformation strategy, be sure to check out their presentation and video.

Second, we are committed to enhancing SAP Cloud ALM’s monitoring capabilities for SAP BTP extensions. By continuously improving these features, we enable our customers to maintain a Clean Core strategy while gaining valuable insights into their solutions’ performance and health. For example, Panasonic has benefitted from SAP Cloud ALM’s ability to integrate local and central observability, including non-SAP components, into the monitoring use cases, and have presented their success story of how they use SAP Cloud ALM to monitor custom-built applications built on BTP Cloud Foundry. Over the next months, we will expand on this success by introducing AI into SAP Cloud ALM: The first use cases are already productively used by our SAP Cloud ALM customers, driving us towards our North Star of Autonomous Application Lifecycle Management. For example, Swisskrono Group is already taking advantage of AI for their alert processing as seen in this presentation and video.

Third, we refined our guidance and support for customers transitioning from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM. The Readiness Check for SAP Cloud ALM, Transition Center, and methodology roadmap is just the beginning; we will continue to develop resources and refine our best practices to make your transition as smooth and efficient as possible. Freudenberg Corporate is taking advantage of these resources as they transition from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM: See the presentation and video.

Lastly, we have increased SAP Cloud ALM development transparency to our customers by publishing a detailed roadmap of planned SAP Cloud ALM innovations on the Road Map Explorer, covering topics such as change and deployment management, test management, document management and solution operations.  

SAP Cloud ALM will continue to improve and evolve; it's a dynamic process where each customer story contributes to shaping a more robust and versatile platform. As we look to the future, our focus will remain on fostering seamless integrations, enhancing user experience, and providing comprehensive support that empowers our customers to thrive in a rapidly changing digital landscape. 

Looking forward to the next 10,000!

entry.title: SAP CRM – Scope Filter Not Working Until Page Refresh (User-Specific Issue)
entry.content:
Hi all,

i'm facing a strange issue in SAP CRM WebUI, specifically in the Request for Change (RfC) screen within the Scope tab.

 

  • When a specific user opens a Request for Change and tries to use the Scope filter, it doesn't return any results initially.

  • However, once the user refreshes the page manually (F5) and tries the same filter again, it starts working properly.

  • From that point on, the filter continues to work as expected during the rest of the session.

  • Other users do not experience this problem.What i tried is as follow:

  • Cleared browser cache
  • Checked user roles/authorizations (seem fine)
  • Tried with different browsers (same behavior).

Has anyone encountered a similar filter or refresh-specific issue in the CRM WebUI or have an idea about this issue.

Best Regards.
